rationale: Ζ-P0..Ζ-P2 now freezes the blocker baseline, the family-by-family prerequisite matrix, and the execution-gate perimeter needed before bounded entry.
The live shard, net, release, trust, and provenance substrate provides real precursor evidence for proof-bearing rehearsal and verification work without honestly proving realization.
The remaining open gaps are now inside the bounded first-wave families and broader Ζ blocker set, not in another missing pre-entry planning layer.
Rollback-bearing staged validation remains the narrowest and most reversible family.
Distributed replay and proof-anchor verification remains admissible only as blocker-reduction verification work.
Bounded runtime cutover proof rehearsal remains admissible only as rehearsal-only and proof-only work under stronger guardrails.
Any additional Υ-D3-style work would now mainly consolidate existing doctrine rather than close a distinct prerequisite gap before bounded entry.
The next honest move is bounded Ζ-A first-wave work followed by an immediate checkpoint, not another pre-entry planning tail.

Broader Ζ remains blocked by replication proof, distributed replay and proof-anchor continuity realization, runtime cutover proof, distributed trust convergence, live trust/publication execution realization, and live receipt-pipeline realization.
Perimeter families such as live shard relocation remain future-only and outside bounded near-term admission.
The repo now has explicit blocker, matrix, and execution-gate surfaces for bounded Ζ-A rather than only broad readiness language.
Ζ-P2 already narrows the admissible perimeter to one admitted family and two admitted-with-cautions families.
Live shard/runtime substrate supports proof-bearing rehearsal and verification more honestly than live transfer, trust execution, or relocation.
cautions: Ready status applies only to the bounded first-wave perimeter frozen by Ζ-P2.
Admission does not authorize live authority movement, live trust/revocation/publication execution, live receipt-pipeline realization, or shard relocation.
The two caution families must stay proof-only and rehearsal-only unless a later checkpoint explicitly widens them.

first_wave_family_findings.title: Rollback-bearing staged transition validation
reason: Rollback, downgrade, release-index, rehearsal, refusal, and generalized receipt/provenance doctrine already provide the strongest upstream law packet.
Server runtime surfaces already expose checkpoint, recovery, deferred-intent, and event-log substrate that can support staged validation honestly.
required_guardrails: staged validation rollback downgrade and refusal-aware rehearsal only
no claim that runtime cutover proof or distributed replay continuity is already closed
no authority handoff state transfer trust execution revocation execution or publication execution

first_wave_family_findings.title: Distributed replay and proof-anchor verification
reason: Shard, cross-shard-log, checkpoint, and replay precursor surfaces justify bounded verification work.
The same surfaces do not realize proof-anchor continuity, quorum semantics, or deterministic replication proof, so the family must stay verification-only.
required_guardrails: verification-only packet work with no live authority movement
no claim that logs ordering or replication policies alone prove lawful distributed continuity
no claim that proof-anchor continuity or quorum semantics are already realized
outputs remain blocker-reduction evidence rather than gate-passage or realization evidence

first_wave_family_findings.title: Bounded runtime cutover proof rehearsal
reason: Checkpoint, recovery, shard lifecycle, event, and intent surfaces justify bounded cutover-proof rehearsal more honestly than abstract planning alone.
Runtime cutover proof, distributed replay continuity, and receipt-pipeline realization remain open blockers, so the family must stay narrower than ordinary cutover wording might imply.
required_guardrails: no authority handoff no state transfer and no live runtime swap claims
explicit proof-packet obligations before any cutover-legality language
no inference of admissibility from runtime protocol or shard substrate alone

rationale: Controller-specific alignment would now mainly consolidate already-frozen doctrine rather than close a distinct blocker before bounded Ζ-A entry.
Defer unless the immediate post-first-wave checkpoint exposes real controller-specific residue.
The remaining work is now inside the first-wave families and their attached blockers, not in another missing pre-entry planning layer.
Another pre-entry planning prompt would mostly restate Ζ-P0..Ζ-P2 without reducing the blocker stack.

zeta_blockers_or_prerequisites.reason: Replication policies and shard substrate exist, but proof-backed replication and lawful state-partition transfer remain unrealized.
Replay, checkpoint, and cross-shard log classes are explicit, but distributed continuity proof and proof-anchor criteria remain open.
Runtime cutover rehearsal can now begin in bounded form, but lawful cutover proof across the frozen boundaries remains unresolved.
Distributed authority foundations and trust prerequisites are explicit, but convergence realization remains absent.
Trust, revocation, and publication doctrine is explicit, but live execution remains outside the bounded first wave.
Generalized receipt law exists, but canonical live receipt-pipeline realization remains future work.
Relocation remains perimeter-only and must not be collapsed into bounded first-wave scope.
Cluster-of-clusters, restartless-core-by-default, and similar families remain beyond bounded near-term Ζ-A.

must_avoid_replacing: the Ζ-P2 first-wave perimeter with convenience widening
doctrinal or gate truth with local traces logs mirrors or runtime naming familiarity
active checkpoint law with stale blueprint numbering or legacy Ζ placeholders

ownership_projection_cautions: fields/ remains canonical semantic field substrate while field/ remains transitional
schema/ remains canonical semantic contract law while schemas/ remains projection-facing
packs/ remains canonical for runtime packaging and activation scope while data/packs/ remains scoped authored-pack authority
canonical prose and semantic specs outrank projected or generated mirrors
the thin runtime/ root is not canonical by name alone
gate freezing must not be loosened by convenience implementation familiarity or bundle/report wording

reason: Ζ-P0..Ζ-P2 completed the exact admission work that had to happen before bounded entry.
The first-wave perimeter is narrow enough to keep work proof-bearing rehearsal-oriented and reversible.
No additional consolidation band is required before bounded entry.

stability.replacement_target: later post-Ζ-A checkpoint may refine readiness judgments without replacing the checkpoint law frozen here
